scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Bhed/Bakri (Sheep/Goat) Palak Uthan Yojana for Scheduled Castes
page_title: Mukhyamantri Bhed/Bakri (Sheep/Goat) Palak Uthan Yojana for Scheduled Castes

details: The "Mukhya Mantri Bhed/Bakri (Sheep/Goat) Palak Uthan Yojana for Scheduled Castes" scheme is introduced by the Animal Husbandry and Dairying Department, Government of Haryana, which aims to uplift Scheduled Caste beneficiaries by providing them with sheep or goat units. Through this scheme, 10 female and 1 male sheep/goat units are provided free of cost. Through this scheme, free sheep or goat units, veterinary assistance, and assistance for purchasing wool are provided to unemployed residents of Haryana. The scheme is implemented by the Haryana Livestock Development Board (HLDB).
Long-term objectives of this scheme are as follows:
To create self-employment opportunities through Sheep and Goat husbandry activities.
To upgrade and develop the Sheep and Goat breeds/germ plasm available in the State.
To increase income generation opportunities for the economically weaker segment of society.
benefits: 20 Ewes and one Ram with Green Fodder cum Salt Block Feeder free of cost.
20 Does and one Buck with Green Fodder cum Salt Block Feeder free of cost.
All animals in the established unit will receive deworming, vaccination, and treatment free of cost by the concerned Veterinary Institution.
Assistance for purchasing wool from the established unit will be provided by the department.
Financial Assistance in form of subsidy for establishment of Sheep/Goat unit
100% subsidy for the establishment of a Sheep and Goat unit of 10 females and 1 male would be provided to the Scheduled Caste (SC).

Initially beneficiary has to bear purchase cost of Sheep and Goat unit by there-own for 25% of the unit cost which will be of ₹17,500/- per unit as margin money (proof for the same should be given by beneficiary), which would be provided to beneficiary account later on as per scheme guidelines.

In the case of SC Category, the Haryana Livestock Development Board (HLDB)/Government will provide financial assistance of 75% of the unit cost (₹52,500/- per unit) on the establishment of the livestock unit after the purchase of livestock to the seller within 15 days, upon submission of all requisite documents.

The financial assistance of balance 25% of unit cost (₹17,500/- per unit) in case of SC beneficiaries would be provided by the HLDB/Government in form of subsidy to beneficiary after two months of establishment of unit subject to verification of unit after purchase of all cases preferably by Deputy Director concerned himself or its representative not below the rank of Sub Divisional Officer/Semen Bank Officer other than purchase committee.
Beneficiaries shall have to purchase a complete/whole Sheep/Goat unit of 10+1 in one slot, and only one beneficiary per household could take the benefit of either Sheep or Goat unit under the scheme.

The financial assistance would be provided to the account of the seller through Concerned Semen Bank officer by the Managing Director, HLDB, out of the head of overall receipt of Milk Cess funds after verification and recommendation of the unit established by the Deputy Director concerned or its representative not below the rank of SDO/ SBO. The financial assistance may be delayed due to the unavailability of the budget or any other unforeseen circumstances.

The beneficiary would be provided veterinary assistance facilities (Treatment, vaccination and consultancy) by the concerned area veterinary institutions free of cost. Assistance for the purchase of wool from an established unit would be provided by the Department.
eligibility: The applicant should be a Scheduled Caste
The applicant should be of 18 to 55 years of age.
The applicant should be a resident of Haryana
The applicant should be a Parivar Pehchaan Patra (PPP).
The applicant should not be employed even in the Private Sector/ Govt. /Semi Govt. Organizations.
There would be no mandatory educational qualifications or training requirements.
Any group/firm/organisation would not be permitted to apply under the Scheme, as the Scheme being individual beneficiary-oriented.
The applicant would have to submit a Self-Declaration in the format annexed as
on the portal for being unemployed and for undertaking to abide by other conditions as required under the Scheme.

online: The eligible applicant may apply online through the Official Portal- Antyodaya-SARAL Portal.
If the applicant is not registered on the portal, he/she gets registered there.
” under ‘Sign In Here’ and fill in all the mandatory details, i.e. Name, Email ID, Mobile Number, Password & State. Click on ‘Submit’.
Verify your mobile number and email ID through the received OTP and complete the registration process.
Login to apply for the scheme:
To avail the benefits of the scheme, visit the Official
and log in through the email ID used while registering.
Enter Password & Captcha and click on ‘Login’.
After successful login, click on ‘Apply for Services’ and then ‘View all Available Services’
Now, you can search for the scheme and click on the scheme to go ahead and fill out the application form.
Enter your Parivar Pehchan Patra number - Family ID and click on “Click here to Fetch Family Data”, which displays the family members registered under the given Family ID.
Select the beneficiary/applicant name and enter the OTP that is being sent to the selected family member for verification. Click on “Click to Verify”
Fill in all the mandatory details and upload all the mandatory documents.
Preview the application form and click on ‘Submit’.
Applicants can track their applications through the Official
by entering their Department Name, Scheme Name, and Application Reference ID.

documents_required: Affidavit regarding return of one male and ten female animals after passing one and half year time from the date of purchase.
Postdated Cheque of ₹ 70,000/- duly signed by the person. (Mandatory).
Availability of space for the construction of shed (Proof required).

faqs.question: What is the Mukhya Mantri Bhed/Bakri Palak Uthan Yojna scheme about?
faqs.answer: Under this scheme, the Government provides 20 Does and one Buck (Goat unit) with Green Fodder cum Salt Block Feeder or 20 Ewes and one Ram (Sheep Unit) with Green Fodder cum Salt Block Feeder free of cost to the beneficiary.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for the Mukhya Mantri Bhed/Bakri Palak Uthan Yojna?
faqs.answer: Scheduled Caste beneficiaries between 18 and 55 years of age who are residents of Haryana and unemployed are eligible.

faqs.question: Are there any educational qualifications or training requirements to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, there are no mandatory educational qualifications or training requirements for this scheme.

faqs.question: Can a group, firm, or organization apply for the Mukhya Mantri Bhed/Bakri Palak Uthan Yojna?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is individual beneficiary oriented, so groups, firms, or organizations are not permitted to apply.

faqs.question: What kind of declaration does an applicant need to submit on the portal?
faqs.answer: The applicant needs to submit a Self-Declaration on the portal stating they are unemployed and undertaking to abide by other scheme conditions.

faqs.question: What benefits are provided to beneficiaries under the Mukhya Mantri Bhed/Bakri Palak Uthan Yojna?
faqs.answer: Beneficiaries receive 20 Does and one Buck (Goat unit) or 20 Ewes and one Ram (Sheep Unit) along with a Green Fodder cum Salt Block Feeder, free of cost.

faqs.question: Is there any provision for returning animals after a certain period?
faqs.answer: Yes, after one year, when the beneficiary's flock size reaches 40-50 animals, 10 female and 1 male animal (Half Unit) will be retrieved from them.

faqs.question: What documents are mandatory for applying to the Mukhya Mantri Bhed/Bakri Palak Uthan Yojna?
faqs.answer: A Scheduled Caste Certificate, Aadhar Card, and PAN Card are mandatory documents.

faqs.question: Is there any affidavit required regarding the return of animals?
faqs.answer: Yes, an affidavit regarding the return of one male and ten female animals after one and a half years from the date of purchase is required.

faqs.question: What is the requirement for the Postdated Cheque?
faqs.answer: A Postdated Cheque of Rs. 70,000/-, duly signed by the person, is mandatory.

faqs.question: Are there any charges other than government, Kendra, and Atal Seva Kendra service charges?
faqs.answer: Based on the provided information, no other charges are mentioned apart from the government charges (No Fee), Kendra Service Charges (₹ 10/-), and Atal Seva Kendra (CSC) Service Charges (₹ 30/-).
